workflow.name: Analyze Meta ads daily with Google Gemini and Google Sheets
workflow.description:
# Analyze Meta ads with Gemini and Google Sheets

**Stop manually exporting CSVs and start automating your marketing insights.**

This workflow is designed for Marketing Agencies, Freelancers, and Media Buyers who want to keep a daily pulse on their Meta (Facebook/Instagram) Ads performance without logging into Ads Manager.

It doesn't just scrape data; it uses **Google Gemini AI** to act as a virtual data analyst. It reviews your campaigns, identifies winning/losing creatives, and writes strategic suggestions for both your agency team and your clients.

### 🚀 What this workflow does
1. **Extracts Data:** Wakes up every morning (6:00 AM) to fetch yesterday's Ad and Campaign performance from the **Facebook Graph API**.
2. **Cleans & Filters:** Automatically ignores paused or zero-spend campaigns to keep your reports clean.
3. **Structuring:** Uses a Code node to group Ads intelligently under their respective Ad Sets and Campaigns.
4. **AI Analysis:** Sends the structured data to **Google Gemini**. The AI analyzes CTR, CPC, and Spend to identify the "Best Performing Ad" and "Worst Performing Ad" per Ad Set.
5. **Reporting:**
   - Saves raw **Campaign Data** to Google Sheets.
   - Saves raw **Ad Data** to Google Sheets.
   - Saves **AI-Generated Insights** (Client & Agency suggestions) to a dedicated sheet.
6. **Error Handling:** If anything breaks (e.g., API token expiry), it instantly sends you an alert via **Gmail** with the error details.

### 💡 Key Features
- **Zero-Spend Filter:** Keeps your spreadsheet tidy by excluding inactive ads.
- **Hierarchical Data Processing:** Groups data logically so the AI understands the context of your tests.
- **Dual-Perspective Insights:** The AI generates two types of advice:
  - *For the Client:* Simple, performance-based updates.
  - *For the Agency:* Technical optimization tips (e.g., "Pause Ad B, Scale Ad A").
- **Robust Error Monitoring:** Includes a dedicated error workflow to notify you of failures.

### 🛠️ Prerequisites
To use this template, you will need:
- **Meta/Facebook Developer App:** A System User Access Token with `ads_read` permission.
- **Google Cloud Console Project:** Enabled APIs for Google Sheets, Gmail, and Vertex AI (Gemini).
- **Google Sheet:** A sheet with three tabs: `Campaigns`, `Ads`, and `AI_Insights`.

### 📝 Setup Instructions
1. **Configure Credentials:** Connect your Facebook Graph API and Google accounts in n8n.
2. **Set Configuration Node:** Open the "Set Configuration" node and paste your `Ad Account ID` and `Email Address` for error alerts.
3. **Link Google Sheet:** Open the three Google Sheets nodes and select your spreadsheet file.
4. **Activate:** Turn on the workflow and let it run daily!

---

**Need help setting this up or want a custom automation for your agency?**
I specialize in building agentic workflows for consultants and agencies.

📧 **Contact me:** bakdaulet.mph@gmail.com

parameters.content:
## Analyze Meta ads with Gemini and Google Sheets

**How it works:**
1.  **Extract:** Fetches Ad and Campaign data from Meta API (Yesterday's data).
2.  **Process:** Aggregates data by Campaign and Ad Set using Code node.
3.  **Analyze:** Sends data to Gemini to generate qualitative insights for both Client and Agency.
4.  **Save:** Exports raw data and AI insights to Google Sheets.

**Setup steps:**
1.  **Configuration:** Update the `Set Configuration` node with your `Ad Account ID` and `Email`.
2.  **Credentials:** Authenticate your `Facebook Graph API`, `Google Sheets`, `Gmail`, and `Google Gemini` credentials.
3.  **Spreadsheet:** Create a Google Sheet and update the `Save...` nodes with your Spreadsheet ID.
4. **Facebook Graph API** [Guide](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1ydWNom0TUVh5SkU9IymlpnM_NXbTqHeL_YetDLn-9m0/edit?usp=sharing)
9. **Google sheets** [template](https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/11M6Co13t9t2P7NtFaTm9VXHnzUHXO_RGYf-FRnHkS28/edit?usp=sharing)
10. Use this format to pull the specific date range: `time_range[since]=YYYY-MM-DD&time_range[until]=YYYY-MM-DD`


Happy setup,
Bakdaulet Abdikhan

PS. Feel free to add slack/email nodes to notify alerts/updates ;)

parameters.subject: =N8N ERROR: Daily Ad Pulse for Ad Account {{ $('Get Config').first().json['Ad Account ID'] }} Failed

parameters.text:
=Analyze the following Meta Ads data and return structured insights.

Data:
{{ JSON.stringify($json.campaigns, null, 2) }}

Rules:
- Always include both campaign-level and adset-level insights.
- Write in a professional, concise tone suitable for client reports.
- For each adset, identify:
  - best_performing_ad
  - worst_performing_ad
  - adset_suggestion (1–2 short sentences)
- For each campaign, summarize overall campaign performance with campaign_suggestion (1–2 sentences).

Return only valid JSON following this schema:

[
  {
    "campaign_name": "",
    "campaign_id": "",
    "adsets": [
      {
        "adset_name": "",
        "adset_id": "",
        "best_performing_ad": "",
        "worst_performing_ad": "",
        "adset_suggestion": ""
      }
    ],
    "campaign_suggestion": ""
  }
]

messageValues.message:
=You are MetaInsightsGPT — an expert Meta Ads performance analyst AI working inside a marketing agency automation system.

Your responsibilities:
- Read structured JSON ad performance data (campaigns → adsets → ads).
- Evaluate ads objectively using spend, CTR, CPC, CPM, and conversions (if available).
- Identify the best- and worst-performing ads per adset.
- Generate useful, data-driven insights:
  - For the agency → what to optimize or scale.
  - For the client → what’s working and why.
- Write in English.

Performance logic:
- Prioritize CTR and conversions as success indicators.
- If conversions missing, use CTR/CPC ratio.
- Consider spend and impressions for significance.
- Ignore zero-impression ads.
- Never use vague words like “good”, “bad”, “try more”.
- Always quantify insights (e.g., “CTR 2.3% vs. 0.7%”).
- Output must be strictly valid JSON (no markdown, no commentary).

You output insights in a consistent JSON structure so they can be parsed into Google Sheets.

parameters.content:
## Configuration & schedule
• Define run frequency
• Set account and reporting parameters

## Campaign-level data collection
• Fetch campaign performance data
• Remove zero-spend campaigns
• Store clean campaign metrics

## Ad-level data collection
• Fetch ad-level performance data
• Normalize and structure records
• Persist raw ad metrics

## AI analysis & insights
• Analyze performance with Gemini
• Detect winners and underperformers
• Generate actionable recommendations

## Insights storage
• Split structured AI output
• Save insights for reporting

## Error handling
• Catch workflow failures
• Send notification email

user.name: Bakdaulet Abdikhan
user.bio: Automation engineer with 2+ years experience. I build custom n8n engines for lead gen, reporting, and sales ops. If an API exists, I can automate it.

data.details: In n8n, when a workflow execution fails, it can start another workflow. This second workflow can be any arbitrary workflow on your n8n instance. Use the Error Trigger node as your Trigger in the Error workflow.

data.details: The Code node allows you to execute JavaScript in your workflow.

The Filter node can be used to filter items based on a condition. If the condition is met, the item will be passed on to the next node. If the condition is not met, the item will be omitted. Conditions can be combined together by AND(meet all conditions), or OR(meet at least one condition).
